name: Define Normal Flow (Special Case Pattern)
source: Clean Code by Robert C. Martin, Chapter 7: Error Handling
problem: Calling code must check for exceptional cases (null, missing values, empty results) at every call site, cluttering business logic with exception-handling conditionals that distract from the normal flow.
steps: Identify places where the caller checks for an exceptional case after each call.
Create a Special Case object that encapsulates the default or null behavior.
Return the Special Case object instead of null or throwing an exception for expected absent data.
Remove the null checks in the calling code, allowing the normal flow to proceed without conditionals.
Apply the Null Object Pattern as a specific form of this principle.
